title: MSME Idea Hackathon 5.0
shortDescription: The MSME Idea Hackathon 5.0, organized by the Entrepreneurship Cell (eCell) of MSIT on 14th August 2025, provided a vibrant stage for innovative thinkers. Held under the Ministry of MSME, it aimed to inspire entrepreneurial ideas addressing real-world challenges. The event united students, faculty, and industry experts in a collaborative atmosphere. Its focus centered on sustainability, technology, and social innovation.
detailedDescription: The MSME Idea Hackathon 5.0 celebrated innovation and creativity, drawing participation from enthusiastic student teams who showcased diverse ideas. The event began with motivating words from Smt. Esha Jakhar and Professor Sandeep Maan, who emphasized innovation, adaptability, and perseverance in entrepreneurship. Academic leaders including Dr. Archana Balyan, Dr. Neeru, and Dr. Rakhi highlighted MSIT’s continued support toward nurturing an entrepreneurial mindset among students.The hackathon featured engaging discussions and impressive project presentations, with Watt Saver AI standing out for its smart energy optimization solution that earned funding support and commendation. The event concluded with a Vote of Thanks by Dr. Neeru Ma’am and a group photograph symbolizing unity and innovation. Overall, the hackathon strengthened the bridge between academia and industry, inspiring students to turn their innovative ideas into impactful ventures.

title: TechForChange-Cohort 4 Ideathon
shortDescription: The Entrepreneurship Cell (eCell) of MSIT, in collaboration with the NASSCOM Foundation, organized the TechForChange-Cohort 4 Ideathon on 22nd August 2025 at Seminar Hall 206. Under the guidance of Convener Neeru Rathee Ma’am, the event offered a platform for students to design innovative solutions to real-world challenges. It promoted creativity, leadership, and technology-driven social innovation among aspiring entrepreneurs. The Ideathon also strengthened industry-academia collaboration through expert mentorship and evaluation.
detailedDescription: The TechForChange-Cohort 4 Ideathon began with an inspiring keynote by Ms. Richa Wahi, founder of TalesDatSpin, who encouraged students to express their entrepreneurial journeys through powerful storytelling. Her talk on clarity and impact in communication set an engaging tone for the day. Following this, the CIENA NASSCOM Ideathon segment commenced, featuring 18 student teams presenting technology-based solutions across fields such as legal tech, agriculture, and social welfare.Judges Akhilesh Kumar Jha and Jaanhavi C. from the NASSCOM Foundation evaluated the teams, offering detailed feedback on innovation, feasibility, and societal benefits. Saffgrow stood out with its impactful agricultural automation idea, earning special appreciation from the panel. The top 10 teams were recognized with ₹2000 Amazon vouchers for their outstanding efforts.The program concluded with a token of gratitude to the guests and judges, celebrating the creativity and enthusiasm of all participants. The event successfully bridged students’ academic skills with real-world entrepreneurial experiences, showcasing eCell MSIT’s dedication to nurturing innovation and social impact through technology.

title: The E-Cell Orientation 2025
shortDescription: The Entrepreneurship Cell (E-Cell) of MSIT organized its Four-Day Orientation Program from 25th to 28th August 2025 at Seminar Hall 206. Led by Convener Dr. Neeru Rathee Ma’am, the event welcomed the new batch with a blend of fun, learning, and interaction. Through engaging activities, creative performances, and team introductions, students were immersed in the vibrant culture of E-Cell. The program aimed to inspire leadership, collaboration, and an innovative entrepreneurial mindset.
detailedDescription: The E-Cell Orientation 2025 set an energetic tone from the outset with a warm welcome, interactive sessions, and insightful addresses from Convener Dr. Neeru Rathee, President Nandini Gangwar, and the core team. Day one featured exciting games like logo guessing and introductions that encouraged bonding and participation among freshers.On the second day, various departments—EVM, PR & Outreach, Design, Technical, Content, and Publicity—outlined their roles, followed by a fun “Guess the Song” challenge and a lively jamming session by Vansh that lit up the hall. Day three brought even more energy with a captivating dance performance, motivational stories, and vibrant participation from all attendees.The final day concluded with creative games, audience engagement, and a standout rap performance by Ojas, adding a memorable touch to the event. Goodies, books, and E-Cell merchandise rewarded active participants, reflecting the organization’s enthusiasm and inclusivity. The program ended with a group photograph, symbolizing togetherness and the start of an exciting journey with E-Cell MSIT.Overall, the four-day orientation successfully instilled creativity, teamwork, and entrepreneurial passion among freshers—perfectly capturing the essence of E-Cell’s mission to empower students as future innovators and leaders.

title: MSME Entrepreneurship Orientation Program
shortDescription: The MSME Entrepreneurship Orientation Program was organized by eCell MSIT under the guidance of Prof. Neeru Rathee on 23rd September 2025 at Seminar Hall 206. The event aimed to encourage entrepreneurship among students and create awareness about various MSME schemes, government initiatives, and financial support systems available for budding entrepreneurs. With enthusiastic participation from students, faculty, and esteemed guests, the event provided deep insights into how the government promotes startups and supports small and medium enterprises in India.
detailedDescription:
The program began with a warm welcome, where the host introduced the dignitaries and set an energetic tone for the session. Prof. Archana Balyan, Director of MSIT, inaugurated the event and highlighted the importance of innovation, creativity, and educational institutions’ role in shaping future entrepreneurs. She encouraged students to explore business opportunities and contribute to the nation's economic growth. Ms. Vishakha, Assistant Director (Gr. II), delivered a detailed overview of the MSME schemes, elaborating on the objectives, benefits, and application process of government grants. She discussed how aspiring entrepreneurs can access funding and support through initiatives like UDYAM registration and other MSME benefit programs. Following her, Shri Sunil Kumar, Assistant Director (Gr. I), shared valuable insights on how to start and sustain a business. He discussed critical elements like business planning, marketing, financial management, and technological innovation. His motivational quote, 'It does not matter how many resources you have; if you don't know how to use them, you will never succeed,' left a strong impression on the audience. Ms. Madhu Malti, Deputy General Manager (Retd.), NSIC, inspired participants by presenting practical business ideas, including ventures like bread making, spice packaging, and soy milk production. She encouraged students to think creatively and use local resources effectively to start profitable enterprises. Shri R.K. Singh, Lead District Manager of the State Bank of India, explained the various financial schemes offered to entrepreneurs, including Mudra loans up to ₹10 lakh, Stand-Up India loans for SC/ST and women entrepreneurs, and other subsidy-based opportunities. He stressed the importance of financial discipline and assured that banks play a supportive role in the journey of entrepreneurship. The event concluded with closing remarks by Prof. Neeru Rathee, who expressed gratitude towards all speakers and participants. A short Q&A session followed, where students asked insightful questions about credit scores and startup funding. The session ended with refreshments and a sense of motivation among the attendees.
Overall, the MSME Entrepreneurship Orientation Program served as a valuable platform for young minds to understand the opportunities and challenges in starting their own ventures. It encouraged students to think beyond traditional career paths and inspired them to take the first step toward becoming successful entrepreneurs.

title: Envisage 4.0 2025
shortDescription: eCell MSIT successfully organized its annual flagship event, Envisage 4.0 2025, on the 31st of October 2025. The event served as a beacon of creativity, innovation, and entrepreneurial spirit, providing a dynamic platform for students to present, discuss, and refine their business ideas under the theme 'Emerge. Explore. Evolve.'
detailedDescription:
eCell Maharaja Surajmal Institute of Technology (MSIT), successfully organized its annual flagship event, Envisage 4.0 2025, on the 31st of October 2025. The event once again served as a beacon of creativity, innovation, and entrepreneurial spirit, providing a dynamic platform for students to present, discuss, and refine their business ideas.

We extend our sincere gratitude to Dr. Neeru Rathee, Convener of E-Cell MSIT, for her continuous guidance, cooperation, and encouragement throughout the planning and execution of the event. Her unwavering support has been instrumental in shaping Envisage into a platform that continues to nurture creativity and inspire innovation. The event was graced by the presence of an esteemed panel of judges, Ms. Harshika Drall, Mr. Shaurya Mishra (Former Vice President of E-Cell MSIT), Dr. Saurabh Trivedi, and Mr. Lakshay Chauhan, each of whom brought their vast experience and unique perspective to the evaluation process.

Adding further inspiration to the day, Mr. Saarthak Bakshi, Entrepreneur and CEO of RISSA IVF, joined as the guest speaker. His presence and powerful insights into entrepreneurship and leadership made the event even more enriching for the aspiring innovators in attendance. The theme for Envisage 2025 'Emerge. Explore. Evolve.' captured the essence of this vision, encouraging participants to think beyond boundaries and redefine possibilities. Powered by Aalisha Institute, the event continued the tradition of nurturing creativity, leadership, and enterprise within the student community.

PITCHING
The 25 shortlisted teams took turns presenting their entrepreneurial ideas before the panel of judges. Each team was evaluated on creativity, feasibility, innovation, and potential social or commercial impact. Participants presented diverse ideas ranging from AI-based solutions, healthcare innovations, and sustainable technologies to creative social ventures addressing educational and environmental issues. The atmosphere in the seminar hall was electric, as every team showcased passion, confidence, and originality in their presentations.

One of the standout projects was CareTales, an AI-powered storytelling platform designed for ill children. This project beautifully combined technology and empathy to bring joy and comfort to young patients through personalized stories generated by artificial intelligence. The concept captured the essence of socially responsible innovation and was highly appreciated by both the judges and the audience.

SPEAKER SESSION
The Envisage event featured an inspiring and thought-provoking session by Mr. Saarthak Bakshi, an accomplished entrepreneur and the CEO of RISSA IVF, who captivated the audience with his remarkable journey in the world of business and innovation. His insights into leadership, strategic thinking, and perseverance left a lasting impression on the participants.

WINNERS DECLARED
After careful evaluation based on innovation, feasibility, impact, and presentation, the judges announced the winners:
1st place - CodeX
2nd place - What the hack
3rd place - Aarogya Catalyst

CONCLUSION
Envisage 4.0 proved to be a monumental success, bringing together like-minded individuals passionate about innovation and growth. The event brought together aspiring entrepreneurs, mentors, and industry professionals, providing a dynamic platform for participants to showcase their innovative business ideas.

title: Eureka – The Marketathon 2026
shortDescription: The Entrepreneurship Cell (E-Cell) of MSIT, under the guidance of Prof. Neeru Rathi, successfully organized Eureka – The Marketathon 2026, an engaging and intellectually stimulating marketing competition aimed at fostering innovation, strategic thinking, and brand-building skills among students.
detailedDescription:
The Entrepreneurship Cell (E-Cell) of MSIT, under the guidance of Prof. Neeru Rathi have successfully organized Eureka – The Marketathon 2026, an engaging and intellectually stimulating marketing competition aimed at fostering innovation, strategic thinking, and brand-building skills among students. The event was hosted by Ms. Preetika from the Event Management Department of E-Cell MSIT. Designed as a dynamic platform for aspiring marketers and entrepreneurs, Eureka encouraged participants to transform ideas into impactful brands while experiencing the intensity of real-world marketing challenges.

The event commenced with a warm welcome to all participants, introducing them to the objectives of the competition and the exciting rewards, including goodies worth ₹5,000. The introduction set an enthusiastic tone for the competition, motivating participants to approach each round with confidence, creativity, and determination.

Objective of the Event
Eureka – The Marketathon was conceptualized to help students understand that marketing extends far beyond selling a product. The event emphasized the importance of understanding customer needs, building strong brand positioning, creating meaningful consumer connections, and strategically presenting ideas in a competitive market environment. Through its structured rounds, the competition aimed to cultivate entrepreneurial thinking, teamwork, adaptability, and effective communication skills. It reinforced the idea that even the most innovative products require well-planned marketing strategies to achieve success.

Marketing Session by Incubation Heads
Before the commencement of the competition, an insightful marketing session was conducted by the Incubation Department Heads Madhav, Satyam, and Krishna. The session provided participants with foundational knowledge and strategic direction, covering key marketing concepts such as brand positioning, audience targeting, strategic formulation, and transforming innovative ideas into impactful brands. Their guidance equipped participants with practical insights, enabling them to approach the competition with structured thinking and clarity. This session ensured that teams were better prepared to align creativity with strategy.

Round 1 – Brand Spirit
Round 1 of the Marketathon, titled “Brand Spirit,” required teams to build a brand based on an on-the-spot problem statement. Each team was given two hours to prepare a structured pitch deck including a brand name, logo, tagline, social media post, and a brief marketing strategy. The round evaluated creativity, originality, storytelling, and strategic alignment with the target audience.

Mentors Anshika, Om Sharma, Hitesh, Rishab, and Vishakha guided participants throughout the preparation phase, ensuring clarity and alignment with marketing principles. Interactive game sessions conducted by Shambhavi and Ansh helped maintain energy and enthusiasm. The round concluded with pitch deck submissions, and shortlisted teams were informed about advancing to Round 2.

Round 2 – Market Mastery Round
Round 2 was designed to elevate the competition by shifting focus from idea creation to execution, communication, and crisis management. The primary objective was to evaluate how effectively participants could present their brand concepts and respond to unexpected business challenges. This stage aimed to nurture confidence, adaptability, logical reasoning, and structured thinking, skills essential in entrepreneurial and corporate environments alike. Round 2 followed a dynamic format with two components: a 90-second Advertisement/Skit Presentation and a Crisis Management Challenge. In the first stage, teams creatively promoted their brand within a strict time limit, focusing on clarity, storytelling, and audience engagement. In the second stage, they were given a business-related crisis scenario and asked to propose practical solutions with strong justification. Teams were evaluated on originality, clarity, execution, feasibility, and logical reasoning.

Judge and Mentorship
A significant highlight of the event was the presence of Aarush Dhawan, Co-Founder, COO & CMO at SERVIZ, who served as the judge for the competition. With extensive experience in brand building, sales strategy, and revenue generation, his participation brought valuable industry perspective to the event. Beyond evaluation, his constructive feedback, insightful observations, and thought-provoking questions provided participants with a deeper understanding of how ideas are assessed in professional settings. His interaction bridged the gap between theoretical marketing concepts and real-world expectations, significantly enriching the learning experience for all participants.

Engagement and Event Atmosphere
Round 2 maintained high energy and enthusiasm throughout the session. The advertisement performances encouraged spontaneity and creativity, while the crisis challenge introduced unpredictability, keeping participants actively engaged. The structured transitions between presentations, appreciation remarks, and supportive audience participation fostered a motivating and competitive environment. The event encouraged students to step outside their comfort zones, present ideas confidently, and handle pressure constructively.

Announcement of Winners
At the conclusion of the competition, the winners of Eureka – The Marketathon 2026 were announced, team “Dhoom Machale” who delivered an exceptional performance. The judge awarded the winning teams with their respective prizes, recognizing their creativity, strategic depth, and confident execution. The announcement marked a celebratory culmination of the event’s competitive spirit and hard work.

Conclusion
Eureka – The Marketathon 2026 proved to be an enriching and transformative experience for all participants. The event successfully combined creativity, strategy, execution, and crisis management into a comprehensive learning platform. Participants demonstrated remarkable innovation, confidence, teamwork, and analytical ability under time constraints.

Regardless of the final results, reaching the advanced stages reflected dedication, preparation, and courage. The event reinforced essential qualities such as communication skills, adaptability, structured reasoning, and leadership potential. The session concluded with gratitude extended to the judge, mentors, organizing team, and participants, marking Eureka 2026 as a significant milestone in fostering entrepreneurial and marketing excellence at MSIT.

title: Study Abroad Seminar 2026
shortDescription: E-cell MSIT has conducted a “Study Abroad” seminar with the objective of guiding students who aspire to pursue higher education overseas under the guidance of E-cell convener Prof. Neeru Rathee. In today’s globalized world, international education has become increasingly significant, offering students exposure to diverse cultures, advanced academic systems, and enhanced career opportunities. Recognizing this importance, the seminar was organized primarily for pre-final and final-year students who are in the crucial phase of making decisions about their future academic and professional paths
detailedDescription: Automatic Zoom Study Abroad Seminar 2026lace: 206, Seminar Hall Date: 16th March 2026, 10:00 am E-cell MSIT has conducted a “Study Abroad” seminar with the objective of guiding students who aspire to pursue higher education overseas under the guidance of E-cell convener Prof. Neeru Rathee. In today’s globalized world, international education has become increasingly significant, offering students exposure to diverse cultures, advanced academic systems, and enhanced career opportunities. Recognizing this importance, the seminar was organized primarily for pre-final and final-year students who are in the crucial phase of making decisions about their future academic and professional paths. The event commenced on a formal and respectful note. Chris Lewis, the guest speaker, was warmly welcomed with a floral bouquet by the President of the Entrepreneurship Cell. Following this, another bouquet was presented to the South Asian representative of IDP, acknowledging their role in supporting students in their journey toward studying abroad. The seminar aimed to provide students with practical insights into international education systems, focusing on the advantages of studying at globally recognized institutions. It also sought to create awareness about how universities abroad integrate academic learning with real- world experience, thereby preparing students for successful careers. The keynote speaker for the seminar was Chris Lewis, who serves as the Executive Director at Swinburne University. He holds a Master of Education (Management and Leadership) from theniversity of Sydney and a Master of Business Management from Monash University. With his extensive experience in the field of higher education, he brought valuable knowledge and perspective to the session. Chris Lewis began his address with a light-hearted remark about his speaking style. He mentioned that he tends to speak quickly, comparing himself to an Australian fast bowler. He explained that once he gathers momentum, it becomes difficult for him to slow down. This humorous analogy immediately captured the attention of the audience and helped create a relaxed and engaging atmosphere. His approachable and energetic style made the session more interactive and less formal, encouraging students to listen attentively. By blending humor with information, he ensured that the audience remained engaged throughout his presentation. 1. Key Points Discussed by the Speaker 1.1 Emphasis on Practical Learning One of the main highlights of Chris Lewis’s presentation was the emphasis on practical learning. He explained that Swinburne University strongly focuses on bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application. Unlike traditional education systems that often prioritize classroom learning, the university ensures that students gain hands-on experience during their course of study. He clearly stated that no student completes their course without acquiring some level of professional experience. This approach ensures that students are not only academically qualified but also possess the practical skills required in the workplace. Such a system prepares students to face real-world challenges with confidence and competence. 1.2 Industry Partnerships Another significant aspect highlighted during the seminar was the university’s collaboration with a vast network of industry partners. Chris mentioned that Swinburne University has partnerships with approximately 2,600 industry brands. These collaborations provide students with numerous opportunities to engage in internships, live projects, and work placements. Through these partnerships, students gain exposure to real-world work environments, which enhances their understanding of their chosen fields. It also increases their employability, as they graduate with both academic knowledge and practical experience. This strong industry connection is one of the key factors that makes the university stand out. 1.3 Career-Oriented Approach Chris Lewis also emphasized that the university adopts a career-oriented approach to education. The curriculum is designed in alignment with industry requirements, ensuring that students develop relevant skills that are in demand in the job market. By integrating professional experience into academic programs, the university helps students build a strong foundation for their careers. This approach not only benefits students academically but also give them a competitive edge in the global job market.

title: eCell MSIT Presents – Start Up Day Special
shortDescription: MSIT’s Entrepreneurship Cell (eCell) successfully hosted an inspiring Start-Up Day Special Session centred on entrepreneurship and emerging startup culture. The session featured Guest Speaker Mr. Shivam Ahuja, Founder and CEO of SkillCircle, who shared valuable insights from his entrepreneurial journey and motivated students to think beyond conventional career paths. Guided by eCell Convenor Dr. Neeru Rathee, the event enabled students to learn, engage, and explore real-world startup opportunities while gaining the confidence to transform their ideas into impactful ventures.
detailedDescription: MSIT’s Entrepreneurship Cell (eCell) successfully hosted an inspiring Start-Up Day Special Session centred on entrepreneurship and emerging startup culture. The session featured Guest Speaker Mr. Shivam Ahuja, Founder and CEO of SkillCircle, who shared valuable insights from his entrepreneurial journey and motivated students to think beyond conventional career paths. Guided by eCell Convenor Dr. Neeru Rathee, the event enabled students to learn, engage, and explore real-world startup opportunities while gaining the confidence to transform their ideas into impactful ventures. Highlights of the Session • Understand the startup ecosystem and where students fit in • Be exceptionally good at one skill to build a strong foundation • Identify a micro-niche and establish a community around it • Leverage college years to experiment, learn and grow • Build strong alumni connections to unlock real opportunities Words of Encouragement by Dr. Neeru Rathee: Dr. Neeru Rathee, eCell Convenor, warmly welcomed the GuestSpeaker, Mr. Shivam Ahuja, along with all attendees. She highlighted the importance of innovation and entrepreneurship in shaping future leaders and encouraged students to actively engage in such sessions to learn, explore ideas, and make the best use of the opportunities provided by eCell. Understanding the Startup Ecosystem Mr. Shivam Ahuja began by explaining the startup ecosystem and how opportunities often depend on the city and environment an entrepreneur operates in. He highlighted that access to resources, talent, networks, and market demand varies across regions, and founders must learn to adapt their strategies accordingly. He further emphasized that individuals are influenced by the people they spend time with, stating that one becomes the average of the five people they interact with the most. Students were encouraged to surround themselves with motivated, growth-oriented individuals to maintain a strong entrepreneurial mindset. Being Exceptionally Good at One Skill: The speaker strongly emphasized that students should aim to be exceptionally good at one core skill instead of being average in multiple areas. Mastery in a specific domain helps build credibility, confidence, and long-term value. He explained that strong skills make founders more reliable in the eyes of investors, clients, and team members. Students were encouraged to continuously practice, upskill, and refine their chosen area to create a solid foundation for entrepreneurial success. Niche, Community, and College Advantage Another key learning from the session was the importance of identifying a micro-niche and building a community around it. Solving specific problems for a focused audience helps startups gain early traction and loyalty. Mr. Ahuja also encouraged students to leverage their college education as a launchpad for experimentation and innovation. Colleges provide safe space to test ideas, access mentors, and build teams. He highlighted the advantage of hiring talent from within the college, as it allows founders to work with like-minded peers who share similar goals and commitment levels. Additionally, the importance of maintaining strong alumni connections was emphasized. Alumni networks can offer mentorship, industry exposure, partnerships, and early-stage opportunities that are invaluable for young startups. Leveraging College Years to Experiment, Learn, and Grow: The speaker highlighted that college years are a crucial phase where students have the freedom to experiment with ideas, learn through trial and error, and develop practical, real-world skills without the pressure of major financial or professional risks. Students were encouraged to actively participate in college activities, work on projects, attend sessions, collaborate with peers, and explore entrepreneurial initiatives. He emphasized that this phase should be used to build confidence, discover strengths, learn teamwork, and gain hands-on experience, as effectively leveraging college life creates a strong foundation for long-term entrepreneurial success and professional growth. Building Strong Alumni Connections: Alumni networks were highlighted as a powerful growth driver for students and early-stage startups. Alumni can provide mentorship, industry exposure, referrals, career guidance, and practical insights based on real-world experience. The speaker encouraged students to actively connect with alumni through college platforms, events, and professional networks, as strong alumni relationships offer long-term support, open doors to valuable opportunities, and significantly accelerate both career progression and startup growth. Conclusion The session concluded with an interactive Q&A, leaving students motivated and inspired to explore entrepreneurship. The discussion provided practical insights into the startup ecosystem, skill development, and strategic thinking, encouraging students to leverage their potential and take confident steps toward building impactful ventures.

title: E-SUMMIT 2026
shortDescription: ECell MSIT, has successfully organized its annual flagship event, E-Summit 2026, a two-day entrepreneurial fest aimed at fostering innovation, creativity, and leadership among students. Hosted at the Maharaja Surajmal Institute of Technology (MSIT), the summit brought together aspiring entrepreneurs, industry experts, and creative minds on a common platform.
detailedDescription: E-SUMMIT 2026 Date: 24th – 25th February 2026  Time: 10:00 a.m. onwards Venue: MSIT Auditorium, 206 Seminar hall ECell MSIT, has successfully organized its annual flagship event, E-Summit 2026, a two-day entrepreneurial fest aimed at fostering innovation, creativity, and leadership among students. Hosted at the Maharaja Surajmal Institute of Technology (MSIT), the summit brought together aspiring entrepreneurs, industry experts, and creative minds on a common platform. The event revolved around the theme “Game of Throne,” symbolizing strategic thinking, leadership, and the pursuit of excellence in the entrepreneurial ecosystem. Through a diverse range of activities including speaker sessions, cultural programs, startup exhibitions, and competitive events, the summit provided participants with opportunities to learn, network, and showcase their talents.OPENING CEREMONY The inaugural session of E-Summit 2026 commenced at 10:00 a.m. at Maharaja Surajmal Institute of Technology under the guidance of convener Dr. Neeru Rathee and eCell faculty coordinators ( Dr. Shaily Malik, Dr. Sitender Malik, Dr. Rakhi Kamra and Dr. Rakesh Chikara). The event began with a warm welcome extended to the participants, faculty members, and distinguished guests. A ceremonial lamp lighting by Prof. (Dr.) Archana Balyan, Director MSIT along with Prof. (Dr.) Naveen Dahiya, Dean MSIT symbolized dedication, perseverance, and the pursuit of knowledge. The proceedings were further enriched by the invocation of Saraswati Vandana, creating a serene and auspicious atmosphere. Dr. Archana Balyan delivered an inspiring address,emphasizing entrepreneurship as a mindset and encouraging students to embrace risks and innovation. Dr. Mandeep Singh highlighted the growing importance of entrepreneurship in driving economic and societal progress, motivating students to become job creators. The session concluded with a recognition ceremony honoring the esteemed guests and a formal acknowledgment of Aalisha Institute for its valuable support. RANGMANCH – CULTURAL PROGRAM (DAY 1 & DAY 2) The Rangmanch Cultural Program, conducted across both days of E-Summit 2026 at Maharaja Surajmal Institute of Technology, served as a vibrant platform for showcasing the artistic talents of students through music, dance, and interactive segments. On Day 1, the program featured a captivating singing performance by Harsh Jha with songs, followed by an energetic dance performance by Tanisha, and a melodious duo act by Pranav and Ruchit. An engaging “Guess the Brand” quiz further added to audience participation. On Day 2, the program began with a musical performance by Dhun Vibers led by Sambhavi, which received enthusiastic audience response. This was followed by a dynamic dance performance by Riya Singh and a soulful piano performance by Shaurya Rao, both of which captivated the audience. The event was judged by Shaurya Mishra, Chief Advisor of eCell, and concluded with Harsh and Bhavyam being declared as the winners. Overall, Rangmanch across both days was marked by diverse performances and strong audience engagement, contributing significantly to the vibrant atmosphere of the summitECHOES OF INFLUENCE – SPEAKER SESSION The speaker session titled Echoes of Influence: Rising Empires Panel Discussion provided an insightful and interactive platform where distinguished panelists — Riya Jain, Founder of StractsbyRiya; Dr. Mitali Jaiswal, Founder of Life Sutra and SuicideFreeIndia; and Dr. Vassundara Nattes, Founder and Chairman as well as a Dadasaheb Phalke Award winner — shared valuable perspectives on entrepreneurship and personal growth. Hosted by Nandini, President of eCell, the session emphasized the importance of focusing on one's own journey rather than comparing with others. The panelists highlighted self-belief, discipline, and continuous learning as essential components of success, while also encouraging students to take initiative and act without waiting for ideal conditions. Entrepreneurship was presented as a journey requiring resilience, a strong mindset, and consistent effort. The session concluded with the felicitation of the panelists, expressing gratitude for their valuable insights and contributions.E-SUMMIT SHARK TANK 2026 The Shark Tank event brought together 15 selected teams who presented their startup ideas before esteemed judges including Dr. Sanjeen Sawhney, Entrepreneur, Harshit Khaneja, Vice President of eCell, and Mr. Kultar Singh along with Mrs. Narendra Kaur.in a competitive and intellectually stimulating environment. The event highlighted innovative concepts addressing contemporary challenges, with standout performances by Skill Drip, a reel-based learning platform aimed at productive skill development, and Safora, a safety system designed for women and children with real-time tracking capabilities. The session provided participants with valuable exposure, constructive feedback, and mentorship, fostering a culture of critical thinking and innovation. The competition witnessed a range of impactful ideas, and after thorough evaluation, Medivault secured the first position while Unsol emerged as the second runner-up, highlighting the strong entrepreneurial potential among participants. STARTUP EXPO 2026 The Startup Expo served as a dynamic platform for aspiring entrepreneurs to present innovative ideas and business models, creating an environment that closely resembled real-world entrepreneurial ecosystems. Organized in the outdoor areas of the campus, the Expo featured a wide range of student-led ventures spanning domains such as technology, social enterprise, and lifestyle solutions. Participants showcased their prototypes and concepts, followed by interactive discussions with evaluators who provided constructive feedback on feasibility, scalability, and market potential. The event attracted significant attention from attendees and played a crucial role in bridging the gap between academic knowledge and practical application. The initiative of facilitating Startup India registration for promising teams further demonstrated the commitment to nurturing entrepreneurial talent beyond the event.KEYNOTE SESSION – MR. VAIBHAV KANWAR The keynote session by Mr. Vaibhav Kanwar, Founder of Clapbox Music provided an insightful narrative of his entrepreneurial journey, illustrating the importance of identifying market gaps and taking initiative. He shared personal experiences from his early ventures, emphasizing responsibility, customer value, and learning from failures. His journey highlighted key lessons such as avoiding the creation of unnecessary problems, adapting to challenges, and prioritizing practical learning over theoretical knowledge. BID TO BUILD The Bid to Build event, conducted in Seminar Hall 206, provided a platform for students to address real-world challenges through innovative solutions. Judged by Mr. Amar Singh Bedi, an Amazon FBA coach, an experienced professional in the field of e-commerce, the event emphasized practical application and strategic thinking. Participants presented diverse ideas, including an AI-enabled mental health solution offering chatbot support, anonymous therapy, and mood tracking, as well as Skilldrip, a platform utilizing short-form content to provide career guidance in emerging fields. Each presentation was followed by detailed evaluation and feedback, enabling participants to refine their concepts. The event successfully encouraged innovation, critical thinking, and entrepreneurial problem-solving among students. The competition concluded with Ace, Baniya Monopoly, and Croissant Capital emerging as the winners, reflecting the creativity and practical thinking of the participating teams.CONCLUSION E-Summit 2026 emerged as a comprehensive and impactful event that successfully integrated entrepreneurship, innovation, and creativity over the course of two days. Through a combination of cultural programs, speaker sessions, startup exhibitions, and competitive events, the summit provided participants with valuable exposure to real-world challenges and opportunities. The event fostered an environment of collaboration, learning, and inspiration, encouraging students to think beyond conventional boundaries and pursue entrepreneurial aspirations. The collective efforts of the organizers, participants, speakers, judges, and sponsors contributed significantly to the success of the summit, reinforcing the commitment to nurturing future leaders and innovators.

title: Vendor Development Programme
shortDescription: A Two-Day Vendor Development Programme (VDP) was successfully organized by the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of India, through the MSME–Development and Facilitation Office (DFO), Okhla, New Delhi, in association with The Toy Association of India, Indian Oil Corporation Ltd. (IOCL), and Maharaja Surajmal Institute of Technology (MSIT). The programme was held on 11–12 March 2026 at Maharaja Surajmal Institute of Technology (MSIT), C-4, Janakpuri, New Delhi. The event was organized under the guidance and support of Prof. Neeru Rathee (Dean Human Resource Development and Alumni Affairs, MSIT and convenor, eCell) and Dr. Manoj Malik, (Dean Associate, National and International Collaboration, MSIT). The programme aimed to strengthen the MSME ecosystem and provide toy manufacturers with opportunities to interact with government procurement agencies and major public sector organizations.
detailedDescription:
A Two-Day Vendor Development Programme (VDP) was successfully organized by the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of India, through the MSME–Development and Facilitation Office (DFO), Okhla, New Delhi, in association with The Toy Association of India, Indian Oil Corporation Ltd. (IOCL), and Maharaja Surajmal Institute of Technology (MSIT). The programme was held on 11–12 March 2026 at Maharaja Surajmal Institute of Technology (MSIT), C-4, Janakpuri, New Delhi. The event was organized under the guidance and support of Prof. Neeru Rathee (Dean Human Resource Development and Alumni Affairs, MSIT and convenor, eCell) and Dr. Manoj Malik, (Dean Associate, National and International Collaboration, MSIT). The programme aimed to strengthen the MSME ecosystem and provide toy manufacturers with opportunities to interact with government procurement agencies and major public sector organizations.
The programme witnessed enthusiastic participation from around 60 toy manufacturers, along with representatives from government departments, public sector undertakings, financial institutions, and industry associations. An exhibition was organized alongside the programme where participating toy manufacturers displayed a wide range of innovative and indigenous toys. This exhibition provided an excellent platform for networking, collaboration, and showcasing the potential of India’s toy manufacturing sector.
The programme commenced with the registration of participants and networking tea, followed by the inauguration of the exhibition and ceremonial lighting of the lamp by the Chief Guest and other dignitaries. The inaugural session included a welcome address by Sh. Sunil Kumar, IEDS, Deputy Director, MSME-DFO, Okhla, who highlighted the importance of vendor development initiatives instrengthening MSMEs and promoting indigenous manufacturing. This was followed by an address by Er. Kaptan Singh, President, Surajmal Memorial Education Society, who emphasized the role of academic institutions in supporting industry and entrepreneurship. He discussed the following points:• Providing internship opportunities for students of MSI and MSIT in the toy industry.
• Creating placement opportunities for MSIT students in the toy manufacturing sector.
• Establishing a Centre of Excellence or Innovation Lab for Toy Development at MSIT to promote research, design, and innovation.
The Chairman of the Toy Association of India agreed in principle to support these initiatives. Dr. R. K. Bharti, IEDS, Director, MSME-DFO, Okhla, presented the aims and objectives of the programme and encouraged MSMEs to actively participate in government procurement systems. Special addresses were delivered by Sh. Rajeev Chhabra, Deputy Commissioner of Industries, Government of NCT of Delhi, and Sh. Ajay Agarwal, President, Toy Association of India, who spoke about the growth opportunities for the toy manufacturing sector in India. The programme also featured addresses by distinguished guests including Sh. Jagdeep Kumar Rana, Executive Director, Indian Oil Corporation Ltd., and Ms. Ashwini Lal, IES, Additional Development Commissioner, MSME. The Chief Guest, Dr. Rajneesh, IAS, Additional Secretary and Development Commissioner (MSME), Ministry of MSME, delivered the inaugural address, emphasizing the Government of India’s commitment to strengthening MSMEs and encouraging domestic manufacturing under the “Make in India” initiative. The event was graced by Smt. Esha Jhakhar (Vice-President, Surajmal Memorial Education Society) and Sh.Rajpal Solanki (Treasurer, Surajmal Memorial Education Society).The event included several technical sessions and presentations by representatives of major organizations and procurement agencies. These sessions provided detailed insights into vendor registration procedures, procurement policies, and eligibility criteria for becoming suppliers to organizations such as Indian Oil Corporation Ltd., Indian Railways (Ircon International Ltd.), NTPC Ltd., CBSE, and Power Grid Corporation of India Ltd. Participants were also given an opportunity to interact directly with officials from CPSUs and MSME departments to clarify their queries and understand procurement processes more effectively.On the second day, technical sessions focused on government schemes and financial support for MSMEs. Presentations were delivered on NSIC schemes for MSMEs, vendor registration procedures for NCERT and Border Security Force (BSF), and the process for buyer and seller registration on the Government e-Marketplace (GeM) portal. Additional sessions covered vendor registration with the Canteen Stores Department (CSD) and financial assistance schemes offered by Union Bank of India and SIDBI. Officials from MSME-DFO also explained various government schemes aimed at supporting MSMEs and promoting entrepreneurship. The programme concluded with a valedictory function and distribution of certificates of participation, followed by high tea. Overall, the Vendor Development Programme proved to be highly beneficial for participating toy manufacturers by providing them with valuable information about procurement opportunities, vendor registration procedures, and financial support mechanisms. The event also strengthened collaboration between government agencies, industry associations, and MSMEs, thereby contributing to the growth and development of the Indian toy manufacturing sector.
